items.title: At least one entry-point convention is present
items.rationale: At least one entry-point convention is present. (no clause matched)
items.fix_hint:
Add a `[project.scripts]` table to pyproject.toml mapping a CLI
name to your `module.cli:main` entry point. For Go, add
`cmd/<name>/main.go`. For Node, set `"bin"` or `"main"` in
package.json. The goal: an agent running `--help` on the package
should find a command that works.

items.title: Large file: index
items.fix_hint:
Split the file into smaller modules grouped by concept, or add an
exclusion in `.agent-readiness.toml` if the file is truly intentional
(large generated tables, etc.).

items.title: README is missing signals for: install
items.rationale: README is missing signals for: install.
items.fix_hint:
Add three short sections to README: Install (one fenced block),
Run (one fenced block showing the canonical entry point), Test
(one fenced block). Three fenced blocks is the floor; if your
project genuinely needs more, write more.

items.title: Hotspot: data/scores
items.rationale: Hotspot: data/scores.json changed 12 times and is >200 lines.
items.fix_hint:
For each hotspot, identify the dominant axes of change (e.g. one
file holds both API routing and validation logic) and split along
those lines. After the split, both halves see lower per-file churn.

items.title: Issue templates, PR template, or CODEOWNERS configured
items.rationale: Issue templates, PR template, or CODEOWNERS configured. (no clause matched)
items.fix_hint:
At minimum: drop `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md` (GitHub has
a wizard), a `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` with a checklist,
and a `CODEOWNERS` file mapping `*` to the team that should review
agent-opened PRs.
